<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Complexity theory</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>

<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}

      throwOnError:false
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();

<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

1 Time & big-O 2 P and NP 3 NP-completeness 4 Reductions 5 PSPACE 6 Hierarchy

Complexity theory

Once we know what is computable, the next question is what is computable cheaply. Time and space, big-O and big-Omega, P and NP, polynomial-time reductions, the Cook–Levin tableau, Savitch's recursive simulation, and the diagonal hierarchy theorems — six concepts that carve the feasible from the infeasible.

Computability fixed the boundary between solvable and unsolvable. Complexity refines the solvable side: how do resource costs (time, memory, randomness, advice) scale with input size? The headline class is $\mathsf{P}$ — problems decidable by a deterministic Turing machine in polynomial time — paired with $\mathsf{NP}$, problems with a polynomial-time verifier. Whether $\mathsf{P}=\mathsf{NP}$ is the deepest open problem in the field. Around this central drama sit the rest of the cast: polynomial-time reductions, NP-completeness, PSPACE, the hierarchy theorems, and the few separations we can actually prove.

Plan. Section 1 fixes asymptotic notation and the time class $\mathrm{DTIME}$. Section 2 introduces $\mathsf{P}$ and $\mathsf{NP}$ via the verifier definition. Section 3 establishes Cook–Levin and the notion of NP-completeness. Section 4 walks the Karp web — how 3-SAT propagates hardness to clique, vertex-cover, Hamiltonian cycle, subset-sum. Section 5 does the same for space: $\mathsf{PSPACE}$, Savitch, TQBF. Section 6 turns the diagonal back on time and space themselves to prove the only strict separations we know.

1. Time complexity and big-O

Fix a multitape Turing-machine model. The running time $t_M(x)$ of $M$ on input $x$ is the number of transitions before $M$ halts. The time class is

\[ \mathrm{DTIME}(f(n)) \;=\; \{\,L : \exists\,M\text{ deciding }L\text{ with }t_M(x)\le f(|x|)\text{ for all }x\,\}. \]

Constants and lower-order terms are absorbed by asymptotic notation:

  • Big-O. $f=O(g)$ iff $\exists c>0,\,n_0$ with $f(n)\le c\,g(n)$ for $n\ge n_0$.
  • Big-Omega. $f=\Omega(g)$ iff $g=O(f)$.
  • Big-Theta. $f=\Theta(g)$ iff $f=O(g)$ and $f=\Omega(g)$.
  • Little-o. $f=o(g)$ iff $f(n)/g(n)\to 0$.
Polynomial vs exponential. $\mathsf{P}=\bigcup_{k\ge 1}\mathrm{DTIME}(n^k)$ is the working notion of feasible. $\mathsf{EXP}=\bigcup_{k\ge 1}\mathrm{DTIME}(2^{n^k})$. The gap between $n^{100}$ and $2^n$ is enormous in practice but feels small on a log-log plot — the widget makes this concrete.

The widget plots five canonical growth rates on log-log axes. On a linear scale, $2^n$ swallows $n^{100}$ instantly; on log-log, polynomials are straight lines (slope $=$ degree) and exponentials are upward-curving (slope $\to\infty$). Drag the slider to see where each curve sits at $n=2^k$.

<script>
/* =========================== Big-O log-log widget =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#bigo-svg'), out=$('#bigo-out'), kIn=$('#bigo-k'), kVal=$('#bigo-k-val');
  const W=620, H=320, padL=60, padR=30, padT=30, padB=40;
  const KMIN=1, KMAX=20;
  // y axis: log2(value), capped at 80 to keep 2^20 = 2^20 visible
  const YMAX = 80;
  // functions: name, color, log2-value at exponent k where n=2^k
  const funcs = [
    {id:'logn', name:'log n',    color:'var(--cyan)',   log2: k => Math.log2(Math.max(1, k))},
    {id:'n',    name:'n',        color:'var(--blue)',   log2: k => k},
    {id:'nlogn',name:'n log n',  color:'var(--green)',  log2: k => k + Math.log2(Math.max(1,k))},
    {id:'n2',   name:'n²',       color:'var(--violet)', log2: k => 2*k},
    {id:'2n',   name:'2^n',      color:'var(--pink)',   log2: k => Math.pow(2, k)}
  ];

  function xPx(k){ return padL + (k - KMIN) / (KMAX - KMIN) * (W - padL - padR); }
  function yPx(v){ return H - padB - Math.min(v, YMAX) / YMAX * (H - padT - padB); }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL, y1:H-padB, x2:W-padR, y2:H-padB,
      stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL, y1:padT, x2:padL, y2:H-padB,
      stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1}));
    // x ticks (every 4)
    for(let k=KMIN;k<=KMAX;k+=4){
      const x=xPx(k);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x, y1:H-padB, x2:x, y2:H-padB+4,
        stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x, y:H-padB+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent = `2^${k}`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // y ticks
    [1,4,16,64].forEach(v => {
      const y=yPx(v);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL-4, y1:y, x2:padL, y2:y,
        stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:padL-8, y:y+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent = `2^${v}`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    // axis labels
    const xl=SVG('text',{x:(padL+W-padR)/2, y:H-6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    xl.textContent = 'input size n  (log scale)';
    svg.appendChild(xl);
    const yl=SVG('text',{x:14, y:(padT+H-padB)/2,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,
      fill:'var(--mute)', transform:`rotate(-90, 14, ${(padT+H-padB)/2})`});
    yl.textContent = 'value f(n)  (log scale)';
    svg.appendChild(yl);

    // plot each function
    funcs.forEach(f => {
      let d = '';
      for(let k=KMIN;k<=KMAX;k++){
        const v = f.log2(k);
        const x = xPx(k), y = yPx(v);
        d += (k===KMIN ? `M${x} ${y}` : ` L${x} ${y}`);
      }
      svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d, fill:'none', stroke:f.color,'stroke-width':2}));
    });

    // marker for current k
    const k = parseInt(kIn.value, 10);
    const cx = xPx(k);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:cx, y1:padT, x2:cx, y2:H-padB,
      stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1, 'stroke-dasharray':'3 3'}));
    funcs.forEach(f => {
      const v = f.log2(k);
      const cy = yPx(v);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx, cy, r:4, fill:f.color,
        stroke:'var(--bg)','stroke-width':1.5}));
    });

    // legend (viewport coords)
    funcs.forEach((f,i) => {
      const ly = padT + 14 + i*18;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:W-padR-110, y:ly-9, width:14, height:3,
        fill:f.color}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-92, y:ly-3,'font-size':12, fill:f.color});
      t.textContent = f.name;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });
  }

  function update(){
    const k = parseInt(kIn.value, 10);
    const n = Math.pow(2, k);
    kVal.textContent = `k=${k} → n=${n.toLocaleString()}`;
    const lines = funcs.map(f => {
      const v = f.log2(k);
      const approx = v >= 60 ? `2^${v.toFixed(0)} (≈ ${v.toFixed(1)} bits)`
                              : Math.round(Math.pow(2, v)).toLocaleString();
      return `${f.name.padEnd(8)} = ${approx}`;
    });
    out.textContent =
      `at n = 2^${k} = ${n.toLocaleString()}:\n\n` +
      lines.join('\n') +
      `\n\nA polynomial $n^d$ on log-log is a line of slope $d$.\n` +
      `An exponential $2^n$ rises off-chart almost immediately.`;
    render();
  }

  kIn.addEventListener('input', update);
  update();
})();
</script>

2. P and NP

A decision problem is a language $L\subseteq\{0,1\}^*$. We ask: given $x$, is $x\in L$?

$\mathsf{P}=\bigcup_k\mathrm{DTIME}(n^k)$ is the class of languages with a polynomial-time decider. $\mathsf{NP}$ is the class with a polynomial-time verifier:

\[ L\in\mathsf{NP} \iff \exists\,V\in\mathsf{P},\,p\in\mathbb{Z}[x]\text{ with } x\in L \iff \exists w,|w|\le p(|x|),\,V(x,w)=1. \]

The witness $w$ is a short certificate that $x$ is a yes-instance, and $V$ checks it in polynomial time.

problemwitness $w$verifier $V(x,w)$
SATsatisfying assignmentplug in, evaluate clauses
CIRCUIT-SATinput settingsimulate gates
HAMILTONIAN-CYCLEcyclic ordering of verticescheck edges + visit each vertex once
CLIQUE$k$-vertex subsetcheck all $\binom{k}{2}$ edges present
SUBSET-SUMchosen subsetadd and compare

Trivially $\mathsf{P}\subseteq\mathsf{NP}$ — a decider is a verifier that ignores the witness. The reverse inclusion is the $\mathsf{P}$ vs $\mathsf{NP}$ question: does every short certificate translate into an efficient algorithm? Almost everyone believes no, but no proof exists.

Equivalent formulation. $\mathsf{NP}=\bigcup_k\mathrm{NTIME}(n^k)$, where $\mathrm{NTIME}(f)$ uses a nondeterministic TM that on each step may branch and accepts iff some branch reaches $q_{\text{acc}}$ within $f(n)$ steps. The branch leading to acceptance is exactly the witness.

The widget shows a verifier in action on a small SAT instance. Pick a witness (a $\{0,1\}$-assignment), and watch the verifier check each clause — green if satisfied, pink if not. The instance is satisfiable iff some witness lights up all clauses.

Polynomial-time verifier for 3-SAT
click each variable to toggle, watch clauses light up

<script>
/* =========================== SAT verifier widget =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#vf-svg'), out=$('#vf-out'), bitsRow=$('#vf-bits');
  const satBtn=$('#vf-sat'), badBtn=$('#vf-bad');
  // formula: (x1 ∨ x2 ∨ ¬x3) ∧ (¬x1 ∨ x2 ∨ x4) ∧ (x1 ∨ ¬x2 ∨ x3) ∧ (¬x2 ∨ ¬x3 ∨ ¬x4)
  // each clause: array of [varIndex, negated]
  const clauses = [
    [[0,false],[1,false],[2,true]],
    [[0,true], [1,false],[3,false]],
    [[0,false],[1,true], [2,false]],
    [[1,true], [2,true], [3,true]]
  ];
  const N = 4;
  let w = [false,false,false,false]; // current witness

  function evalClause(c){
    return c.some(([i, neg]) => neg ? !w[i] : w[i]);
  }
  function clauseStr(c){
    return c.map(([i,neg]) => (neg?'¬':'') + 'x' + (i+1)).join(' ∨ ');
  }

  function buildBits(){
    bitsRow.innerHTML = '';
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      const b = document.createElement('button');
      b.textContent = `x${i+1}=${w[i]?1:0}`;
      b.addEventListener('click', () => { w[i] = !w[i]; render(); });
      bitsRow.appendChild(b);
    }
  }

  function render(){
    // refresh bit buttons text
    [...bitsRow.children].forEach((b,i) => {
      b.textContent = `x${i+1}=${w[i]?1:0}`;
      b.classList.toggle('active', w[i]);
    });
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=240, padL=30, padT=20;
    const cellH = 42;
    // header
    const h = SVG('text',{x:padL, y:padT,'font-size':12, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    h.textContent = 'φ(x) = (x1 ∨ x2 ∨ ¬x3) ∧ (¬x1 ∨ x2 ∨ x4) ∧ (x1 ∨ ¬x2 ∨ x3) ∧ (¬x2 ∨ ¬x3 ∨ ¬x4)';
    svg.appendChild(h);

    // clauses
    let allTrue = true;
    clauses.forEach((c, i) => {
      const y = padT + 30 + i*cellH;
      const ok = evalClause(c);
      if(!ok) allTrue = false;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:padL, y, width:W-2*padL, height:cellH-8, rx:6,
        fill: ok ? 'rgba(131,193,103,0.10)' : 'rgba(224,122,95,0.10)',
        stroke: ok ? 'var(--green)' : 'var(--pink)','stroke-width':1.5}));
      // clause text
      const t = SVG('text',{x:padL+14, y:y+cellH/2 - 1,'font-size':14,
        'font-family':'ui-monospace,monospace', fill:'var(--ink)'});
      t.textContent = `C${i+1}: (${clauseStr(c)})`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
      // literal evals
      let lx = padL + 250;
      c.forEach(([vi, neg]) => {
        const lv = neg ? !w[vi] : w[vi];
        const fill = lv ? 'var(--green)' : 'var(--mute)';
        const t2 = SVG('text',{x:lx, y:y+cellH/2 - 1,'font-size':12,
          'font-family':'ui-monospace,monospace', fill});
        t2.textContent = `${neg?'¬':''}x${vi+1}=${lv?1:0}`;
        svg.appendChild(t2);
        lx += 70;
      });
      // verdict
      const verd = SVG('text',{x:W-padL-14, y:y+cellH/2-1,'text-anchor':'end',
        'font-size':14,'font-weight':700, fill: ok?'var(--green)':'var(--pink)'});
      verd.textContent = ok ? '✓ satisfied' : '✗ unsatisfied';
      svg.appendChild(verd);
    });

    // overall verdict bar
    const vy = padT + 30 + clauses.length*cellH + 4;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:padL, y:vy, width:W-2*padL, height:24, rx:6,
      fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke: allTrue?'var(--green)':'var(--pink)','stroke-width':2}));
    const v = SVG('text',{x:W/2, y:vy+16,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13,
      'font-weight':700, fill: allTrue?'var(--green)':'var(--pink)'});
    v.textContent = allTrue ? `V(φ, w) = 1  → witness accepted` : `V(φ, w) = 0  → some clause unsatisfied`;
    svg.appendChild(v);

    // readout
    const wstr = w.map(b => b?1:0).join('');
    const checks = clauses.map((c,i) => `C${i+1}: ${evalClause(c)?'✓':'✗'}`).join('  ');
    out.textContent =
      `witness w = ${wstr}\n` +
      `clauses   : ${checks}\n` +
      `verdict   : ${allTrue?'φ is satisfied — w is a valid certificate':'rejected — try another assignment'}\n\n` +
      `The verifier ran in O(|φ|) time. NP = problems with such a verifier.`;
  }

  satBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ w=[true,true,true,true]; render(); });
  badBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ w=[false,false,true,true]; render(); });

  buildBits();
  render();
})();
</script>

3. NP-completeness and Cook–Levin

A language $L$ is NP-hard (under polynomial-time many-one reductions) if every $A\in\mathsf{NP}$ admits a polynomial-time computable $f$ with $x\in A \iff f(x)\in L$. It is NP-complete if additionally $L\in\mathsf{NP}$. NP-complete problems are the hardest in $\mathsf{NP}$: a polynomial-time algorithm for any one would collapse $\mathsf{P}=\mathsf{NP}$.

Cook (1971), Levin (1973). SAT — boolean satisfiability — is NP-complete.

The proof builds, from any nondeterministic polynomial-time machine $M$ and input $x$, a polynomial-size CNF $\varphi_{M,x}$ such that $\varphi_{M,x}$ is satisfiable iff $M$ accepts $x$. The construction lays out a tableau: rows are time steps $0,1,\ldots,T(n)$, columns are tape cells $1,\ldots,T(n)$, each cell holds a symbol or a (state, symbol) pair when the head is there. Variables $z_{i,j,s}$ encode "cell $j$ at time $i$ holds $s$". Clauses force:

  • each cell holds exactly one symbol (uniqueness);
  • row $0$ matches the input $x$ (initialisation);
  • each $2\times 3$ window in adjacent rows respects $\delta$ (transition);
  • some row reaches $q_{\text{acc}}$ (acceptance).

Each constraint is a constant-size clause; total size is $\mathrm{poly}(T(n))=\mathrm{poly}(n)$. A satisfying assignment is exactly an accepting computation history.

The widget below shows a small tableau for a 2-step nondeterministic computation. Each row is a configuration; each $2\times 3$ window highlighted in yellow is one transition constraint. Toggle "show variables" to see the encoding $z_{i,j,s}$.

Cook–Levin tableau
click a window to highlight its transition clause

<script>
/* =========================== Cook-Levin tableau widget =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#tab-svg'), out=$('#tab-out');
  // 4 time steps, 6 cells. show a tiny computation: input "11", step right, halt.
  const ROWS=4, COLS=6;
  // each cell: symbol or (state,symbol) pair, displayed
  const tableau = [
    ['(q0,1)','1',  '_',  '_',  '_', '_'],
    ['1',     '(q1,1)','_','_', '_', '_'],
    ['1',     '1',  '(q1,_)','_','_','_'],
    ['1',     '1',  '_',  '(qA,_)','_','_']
  ];
  let highlight = null;  // {row, col} of top-left of 2x3 window

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=320;
    const padL=30, padT=30, cellW=80, cellH=46;
    // row labels
    for(let i=0;i<ROWS;i++){
      const y = padT + i*cellH + cellH*0.6;
      const t = SVG('text',{x:padL-10, y, 'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent = `t=${i}`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // col headers
    for(let j=0;j<COLS;j++){
      const x = padL + j*cellW + cellW/2;
      const t = SVG('text',{x, y:padT-8, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent = `j=${j+1}`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // cells
    for(let i=0;i<ROWS;i++){
      for(let j=0;j<COLS;j++){
        const x = padL + j*cellW;
        const y = padT + i*cellH;
        const sym = tableau[i][j];
        const isState = sym.startsWith('(');
        svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x, y, width:cellW-4, height:cellH-4, rx:4,
          fill: isState ? 'rgba(255,216,102,0.10)' : 'var(--panel2)',
          stroke: isState ? 'var(--yellow)' : 'var(--line)','stroke-width':1,
          style:'cursor:pointer','data-i':i,'data-j':j}));
        const t = SVG('text',{x:x+(cellW-4)/2, y:y+(cellH-4)*0.62,'text-anchor':'middle',
          'font-size':12,'font-family':'ui-monospace,monospace',
          fill: isState ? 'var(--yellow)' : 'var(--ink)','pointer-events':'none'});
        t.textContent = sym;
        svg.appendChild(t);
      }
    }

    // highlight 2x3 window if set
    if(highlight){
      const {i, j} = highlight;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{
        x:padL + j*cellW - 3, y:padT + i*cellH - 3,
        width: 3*cellW - 4 + 6, height: 2*cellH - 4 + 6, rx:6,
        fill:'none', stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':3, 'stroke-dasharray':'5 4',
        'pointer-events':'none'}));
    }

    // legend
    const lY = H - 10;
    const it1 = SVG('text',{x:padL, y:lY,'font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    it1.textContent = 'yellow cells = (state, head-symbol)   ·   click a cell to set the 2×3 transition window';
    svg.appendChild(it1);

    // readout
    let detail;
    if(highlight){
      const {i, j} = highlight;
      const top = tableau[i].slice(j, j+3).join(' ');
      const bot = tableau[i+1] ? tableau[i+1].slice(j, j+3).join(' ') : '(off table)';
      detail =
        `2×3 window at rows t=${i},${i+1}, cols j=${j+1}..${j+3}:\n\n` +
        `  ${top}\n  ${bot}\n\n` +
        `Cook–Levin clause: this window respects δ.\n` +
        `Encoded as a constant-size CNF over the variables\n` +
        `z_{${i},${j+1},·}, z_{${i},${j+2},·}, z_{${i},${j+3},·},\n` +
        `z_{${i+1},${j+1},·}, z_{${i+1},${j+2},·}, z_{${i+1},${j+3},·}.`;
    } else {
      detail =
        `Tableau for a tiny nondeterministic TM run.\n\n` +
        `Variables z_{i,j,s} = "cell j at time i holds symbol s".\n` +
        `Clauses force: row 0 = input  ·  each cell has one symbol  ·  every 2×3 window respects δ  ·  some row contains qA.\n\n` +
        `Total CNF size = poly(T(n)).  Satisfiable iff M accepts x.\n` +
        `Click any cell to inspect a window.`;
    }
    out.textContent = detail;
  }

  svg.addEventListener('click', e => {
    const ti = e.target.getAttribute && e.target.getAttribute('data-i');
    const tj = e.target.getAttribute && e.target.getAttribute('data-j');
    if(ti===null || ti===undefined || ti==='') return;
    const i = parseInt(ti, 10), j = parseInt(tj, 10);
    if(i >= ROWS-1) { highlight = null; render(); return; }
    if(j > COLS-3) { highlight = null; render(); return; }
    highlight = (highlight && highlight.i===i && highlight.j===j) ? null : {i, j};
    render();
  });
  render();
})();
</script>

4. Karp reductions and complete problems

Once SAT is NP-complete, the cheapest way to show another problem $L$ is NP-complete is to find any known NP-complete $A$ with $A\le_p L$. Karp's 1972 paper kicked off the bestiary by reducing SAT to 21 natural combinatorial problems; today the catalogue runs to thousands.

Two illustrative reductions:

  • 3-SAT $\le_p$ CLIQUE. Given a 3-CNF $\varphi$ with $m$ clauses, build a graph $G$: one vertex per literal in each clause, edges between literals from different clauses unless they are negations of each other. A satisfying assignment picks one literal per clause, all mutually consistent — exactly an $m$-clique.
  • CLIQUE $\le_p$ INDEPENDENT-SET. Take the complement graph $\overline G$. A clique in $G$ is an independent set in $\overline G$ of the same size. (Trivially also $\le_p$ in the other direction.)
Transitivity. $\le_p$ is closed under composition: if $f$ and $g$ are polynomial-time, so is $g\circ f$. So the Karp web propagates hardness wherever an arrow lands.

The widget shows the standard "Karp tree" rooted at SAT. Click any arrow to read the reduction; nodes light up in pink as they inherit hardness from SAT.

Karp reduction graph
click an arrow to read the reduction

<script>
/* =========================== Karp graph widget =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#karp-svg'), out=$('#karp-out');
  const nodes = {
    SAT:    {x:310, y:50,  label:'SAT',         color:'var(--pink)',   long:'boolean satisfiability'},
    TSAT:   {x:310, y:130, label:'3-SAT',       color:'var(--pink)',   long:'each clause has 3 literals'},
    CLI:    {x:140, y:220, label:'CLIQUE',      color:'var(--violet)', long:'does G have a k-clique?'},
    IS:     {x:300, y:220, label:'IND-SET',     color:'var(--violet)', long:'k-vertex independent set'},
    VC:     {x:460, y:220, label:'VERT-COVER',  color:'var(--violet)', long:'k-vertex cover'},
    HAM:    {x:120, y:310, label:'HAM-CYC',     color:'var(--cyan)',   long:'Hamiltonian cycle'},
    SS:     {x:300, y:310, label:'SUBSET-SUM',  color:'var(--yellow)', long:'subset summing to t'},
    COL:    {x:480, y:310, label:'3-COLOR',     color:'var(--blue)',   long:'proper 3-coloring'}
  };
  // edges: A -> B means A ≤_p B; arrow points from easier-known-NP-hard to harder.
  // For Karp tree rooted at SAT, all flow downward.
  const edges = [
    {from:'SAT',  to:'TSAT', text:'Replace each long clause by gadget clauses + auxiliary variables, e.g. $(\\ell_1 \\lor \\ell_2 \\lor \\ell_3 \\lor \\ell_4) \\equiv (\\ell_1 \\lor \\ell_2 \\lor y) \\land (\\bar y \\lor \\ell_3 \\lor \\ell_4)$. Polynomial blow-up; same satisfiability.'},
    {from:'TSAT', to:'CLI',  text:'For each 3-clause make a triangle of three vertices labelled by the literals. Edges: between literals in different clauses, but only when they are not negations. A k-clique = one literal per clause, mutually consistent = satisfying assignment.'},
    {from:'TSAT', to:'COL',  text:'Build a graph with a "palette" triangle T,F,B and gadgets per clause forcing some literal to be coloured T. A 3-colouring exists iff φ is satisfiable.'},
    {from:'CLI',  to:'IS',   text:'A k-clique in G = a k-independent-set in the complement $\\bar G$. Compute $\\bar G$ in polynomial time.'},
    {from:'IS',   to:'VC',   text:'$S$ is independent iff $V\\setminus S$ is a vertex cover. So IND-SET($G$, $k$) = VERT-COVER($G$, $|V|-k$).'},
    {from:'TSAT', to:'HAM',  text:'Standard "exclusive-or" gadgets: one variable-component per $x_i$, one clause-component per $C_j$. A Hamiltonian cycle traverses each variable component left-to-right (true) or right-to-left (false), visiting each clause node iff that literal satisfies the clause.'},
    {from:'TSAT', to:'SS',   text:'Encode each variable and clause as a digit-position; weights are large numbers whose digit-sum at each position counts truth/clause-satisfaction. The target sum is hit iff the assignment satisfies every clause.'}
  ];
  let highlight = null;

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    // edges
    edges.forEach((e,i) => {
      const a = nodes[e.from], b = nodes[e.to];
      const isHL = (highlight === i);
      drawArrow(svg, [a.x, a.y], [b.x, b.y], {
        color: isHL ? 'var(--cyan)' : 'var(--mute)',
        markerId: 'k-'+i,
        width: isHL ? 2.4 : 1.4
      });
      // invisible thick path for click
      const dx=b.x-a.x, dy=b.y-a.y, L=Math.hypot(dx,dy)||1;
      const ux=dx/L, uy=dy/L;
      const ax=a.x+ux*22, ay=a.y+uy*22;
      const bx=b.x-ux*22, by=b.y-uy*22;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{
        d:`M${ax} ${ay} L ${bx} ${by}`,
        stroke:'transparent','stroke-width':16,
        style:'cursor:pointer','data-edge':String(i)
      }));
    });
    // nodes
    Object.values(nodes).forEach(n => {
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:n.x, cy:n.y, r:30, fill:'var(--panel2)',
        stroke:n.color,'stroke-width':2}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:n.x, y:n.y+5,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,
        'font-weight':700, fill:n.color,'pointer-events':'none'});
      t.textContent = n.label;
      svg.appendChild(t);
      const t2 = SVG('text',{x:n.x, y:n.y+50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,
        fill:'var(--mute)','pointer-events':'none'});
      t2.textContent = n.long;
      svg.appendChild(t2);
    });
    // legend
    const t = SVG('text',{x:30, y:340,'font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)','font-style':'italic'});
    t.textContent = 'A → B  means  A ≤_p B  (B is at least as hard as A).  All nodes inherit NP-hardness from SAT.';
    svg.appendChild(t);

    // readout
    if(highlight !== null){
      const e = edges[highlight];
      out.textContent = `${e.from} ≤_p ${e.to}\n\n${e.text.replace(/\\\\/g,'\\').replace(/\$/g,'')}`;
    } else {
      out.textContent =
        `click an arrow to read the reduction.\n\n` +
        `Karp's web rooted at SAT: SAT ≤_p 3-SAT, then 3-SAT branches to clique, colouring, ` +
        `Hamiltonian cycle, subset-sum.  Each ≤_p arrow is polynomial-time-computable.\n\n` +
        `Once any one of these admits a polynomial-time algorithm, all of NP collapses into P.`;
    }
  }

  svg.addEventListener('click', e => {
    const idx = e.target.getAttribute && e.target.getAttribute('data-edge');
    if(idx === null || idx === undefined || idx === '') return;
    const k = parseInt(idx, 10);
    highlight = (highlight === k) ? null : k;
    render();
  });
  render();
})();
</script>

5. Space complexity and PSPACE

Define $\mathrm{DSPACE}(f)$ and $\mathrm{NSPACE}(f)$ analogously to time, but counting the number of distinct tape cells touched (excluding the read-only input tape — so sub-linear space classes are meaningful). The standard hierarchy is

\[ \mathsf{L} = \mathrm{DSPACE}(\log n) \subseteq \mathsf{NL} = \mathrm{NSPACE}(\log n) \subseteq \mathsf{P} \subseteq \mathsf{NP} \subseteq \mathsf{PSPACE} = \bigcup_k\mathrm{DSPACE}(n^k) \subseteq \mathsf{EXP}. \]

The simulation $\mathsf{P}\subseteq\mathsf{PSPACE}$ is trivial; $\mathsf{NP}\subseteq\mathsf{PSPACE}$ comes from enumerating all witnesses one at a time. The space side has a striking equality:

Savitch (1970). For $f(n)\ge\log n$, $\;\mathrm{NSPACE}(f)\subseteq\mathrm{DSPACE}(f^2)$. Hence $\mathsf{PSPACE}=\mathsf{NPSPACE}$.

Idea. A nondeterministic $f$-space machine has at most $2^{O(f)}$ configurations. To test whether $C_{\text{start}}$ reaches $C_{\text{accept}}$ in $\le 2^k$ steps, recursively ask whether some midpoint $C'$ is reachable in $\le 2^{k-1}$ steps from $C_{\text{start}}$ and from $C'$ to $C_{\text{accept}}$. Each level of recursion uses $O(f)$ space; depth is $O(f)$; total $O(f^2)$.

The canonical PSPACE-complete problem is TQBF — given a fully quantified boolean formula $\forall x_1\exists x_2\cdots Q_n x_n\,\varphi$, is it true? The alternating quantifiers correspond to alternating polynomial-space computation. Two-player games with polynomial-length play (geography, Reversi, generalised hex) reduce to TQBF.

The widget visualises Savitch: a configuration graph with $2^k$ nodes, and the recursive midpoint search that decides reachability in $O(k^2)$ space.

Savitch's recursive midpoint
drag $k$ to grow the search depth
k=3 → up to 2³ steps

Used in
Complexity theory · Time and space hierarchy theorems

<script>
/* =========================== Savitch widget =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#sv-svg'), out=$('#sv-out'), kIn=$('#sv-k'), kVal=$('#sv-k-val');

  function render(){
    const k = parseInt(kIn.value, 10);
    kVal.textContent = `k=${k} → up to 2^${k} = ${1<<k} steps`;
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=280;
    const padL=30, padT=20;
    const usableH = H - padT - 40;
    const levels = k + 1;  // recursion levels 0..k
    const levelH = usableH / levels;

    // Each level shows the segments at that depth.
    // depth d has 2^d segments dividing [0, 2^k].
    for(let d=0; d<=k; d++){
      const numSegs = 1 << d;
      const segLen = (W - 2*padL) / numSegs;
      const y = padT + d*levelH + levelH*0.5;
      // draw segments
      for(let s=0; s<numSegs; s++){
        const x1 = padL + s*segLen;
        const x2 = x1 + segLen;
        // segment line
        svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1, y1:y, x2, y2:y,
          stroke: d===0 ? 'var(--cyan)' : 'var(--violet)',
          'stroke-width': d===0 ? 3 : 2, opacity: 0.4 + 0.5*d/k}));
        // endpoint dots
        svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:x1, cy:y, r:3, fill:'var(--ink)'}));
        if(s===numSegs-1) svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:x2, cy:y, r:3, fill:'var(--ink)'}));
        // midpoint dot for splitting
        if(d < k){
          const mx = (x1+x2)/2;
          svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:mx, cy:y, r:4, fill:'var(--yellow)',
            stroke:'var(--bg)','stroke-width':1.5}));
        }
      }
      // depth label
      const lbl = SVG('text',{x:padL-8, y:y+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      lbl.textContent = `d=${d}`;
      svg.appendChild(lbl);
    }
    // bracket the start/accept at level 0
    const sLbl = SVG('text',{x:padL, y:padT+8,'font-size':11, fill:'var(--cyan)','font-weight':600});
    sLbl.textContent = 'C_start';
    svg.appendChild(sLbl);
    const aLbl = SVG('text',{x:W-padL, y:padT+8,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,
      fill:'var(--cyan)','font-weight':600});
    aLbl.textContent = 'C_accept';
    svg.appendChild(aLbl);

    // bottom legend
    const lg1 = SVG('text',{x:padL, y:H-22,'font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    lg1.textContent = `each yellow dot is a guessed midpoint;  recursion depth = ${k};  per-level space = O(f)`;
    svg.appendChild(lg1);
    const lg2 = SVG('text',{x:padL, y:H-8,'font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    lg2.textContent = `total space  = depth × per-level  = O(f) × O(f) = O(f²)`;
    svg.appendChild(lg2);

    out.textContent =
      `Reach(C_start, C_accept, 2^${k}):\n` +
      `  for each guess C_mid:\n` +
      `    Reach(C_start, C_mid, 2^${k-1}) and Reach(C_mid, C_accept, 2^${k-1})\n\n` +
      `Each level reuses the same O(f) workspace.\n` +
      `Recursion stack has depth ${k+1}, so total space = O(f²).\n\n` +
      `Corollary: NPSPACE = PSPACE.`;
  }

  kIn.addEventListener('input', render);
  render();
})();
</script>

6. Time and space hierarchy theorems

Most separations between complexity classes are open ($\mathsf{P}$ vs $\mathsf{NP}$, $\mathsf{NL}$ vs $\mathsf{P}$, $\mathsf{NP}$ vs $\mathsf{PSPACE}$). The hierarchy theorems are the few separations we can prove — and they all come from diagonalisation.

Time hierarchy (Hartmanis–Stearns 1965). If $f, g$ are time-constructible and $f(n)\log f(n) = o(g(n))$, then $\mathrm{DTIME}(f)\subsetneq\mathrm{DTIME}(g)$.

Sketch. Build $D$ that on input $\langle M\rangle$ simulates $M$ on $\langle M\rangle$ for at most $g(n)$ steps; if $M$ accepts within budget, $D$ rejects, and vice versa. The simulation overhead is the $\log f$ factor (universal-TM bookkeeping). $D$ runs in time $g$, so $L(D)\in\mathrm{DTIME}(g)$. If $L(D)\in\mathrm{DTIME}(f)$, some $M_0$ deciding it has time $\le f(n)$, hence $D$ disagrees with $M_0$ on $\langle M_0\rangle$ — contradiction.

Space hierarchy. If $f, g$ space-constructible with $f=o(g)$, $\;\mathrm{DSPACE}(f)\subsetneq\mathrm{DSPACE}(g)$ (no $\log$ factor — universal simulation in space is constant overhead).

Concrete separations:

  • $\mathsf{P}=\bigcup_k\mathrm{DTIME}(n^k)\;\subsetneq\;\mathrm{DTIME}(2^n)\subseteq\mathsf{EXP}$ — because $n^k\log n^k = o(2^n)$ for every $k$.
  • $\mathsf{L}=\mathrm{DSPACE}(\log n)\;\subsetneq\;\mathsf{PSPACE}$ — by space hierarchy with $f=\log n$, $g=n$.
  • $\mathsf{NL}\subsetneq\mathsf{NPSPACE}=\mathsf{PSPACE}$ — combining space hierarchy and Savitch.

What the hierarchy theorems do not give us is any separation across the determinism/nondeterminism boundary, or any "natural" separation: a polynomial-time language can be hard for $\mathrm{DTIME}(n^{100})$ but easy for $\mathrm{DTIME}(n^{200})$ — but no such language is known to be useful.

The widget below visualises the diagonal: rows are machines $M_0, M_1, \ldots$ in $\mathrm{DTIME}(f)$, columns are inputs, and the cell shows what $M_i$ outputs on $\langle M_i\rangle$ within budget $g$. $D$ flips the diagonal — disagreeing with each $M_i$ at one point.

Hierarchy diagonal

<script>
/* =========================== Hierarchy diagonal widget =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#dg-svg'), out=$('#dg-out'), gapIn=$('#dg-gap'), gapVal=$('#dg-gap-val');
  const N = 6;
  // Each M_i has its own time budget f_i; D has budget g.
  // We pick deterministic answers M_i(j) so the table is reproducible.
  function answer(i, j){ return ((i*7 + j*3 + 5) % 5) < 3; }

  function render(){
    const gap = parseInt(gapIn.value, 10);
    gapVal.textContent = `×${gap}`;
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=320, padL=120, padT=40, cell=44;
    // column headers (input length n_j)
    for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
      const t = SVG('text',{x:padL + cell/2 + j*cell, y:padT-12,'text-anchor':'middle',
        'font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent = `〈M${j}〉`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // row labels
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      const ty = padT + cell/2 + i*cell + 5;
      const lbl = SVG('text',{x:padL-12, y:ty,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,
        fill:'var(--violet)','font-weight':600});
      lbl.textContent = `M${i}  (f budget)`;
      svg.appendChild(lbl);
      for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
        const x = padL + j*cell, y = padT + i*cell;
        const onDiag = (i === j);
        const v = answer(i, j);
        svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x, y, width:cell, height:cell, rx:4,
          fill: onDiag ? 'rgba(255,216,102,0.18)' : 'var(--panel2)',
          stroke: onDiag ? 'var(--yellow)' : 'var(--line)','stroke-width': onDiag ? 2 : 1}));
        const m = SVG('text',{x:x+cell/2, y:y+cell*0.62,'text-anchor':'middle',
          'font-size':14,'font-weight':700,
          fill: v ? 'var(--green)' : 'var(--pink)','pointer-events':'none'});
        m.textContent = v ? '1' : '0';
        svg.appendChild(m);
      }
    }
    // D row
    const dy = padT + N*cell + 12;
    const lbl = SVG('text',{x:padL-12, y:dy+cell/2+5,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,
      fill:'var(--cyan)','font-weight':700});
    lbl.textContent = `D  (g = ${gap}·f budget)`;
    svg.appendChild(lbl);
    for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
      const x = padL + j*cell, y = dy;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x, y, width:cell, height:cell, rx:4,
        fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':1.5}));
      const dv = !answer(j, j);  // D flips the diagonal
      const m = SVG('text',{x:x+cell/2, y:y+cell*0.62,'text-anchor':'middle',
        'font-size':14,'font-weight':700, fill: dv ? 'var(--green)' : 'var(--pink)'});
      m.textContent = dv ? '1' : '0';
      svg.appendChild(m);
    }
    // dropped-budget annotation
    const a = SVG('text',{x:padL + N*cell + 16, y:dy+cell/2+5,'font-size':11,
      fill:'var(--mute)','font-style':'italic'});
    a.textContent = `D needs ≥ ${gap}× the f budget to simulate M_i + flip`;
    svg.appendChild(a);

    // legend (viewport coords)
    const lY = H - 12;
    const items = [
      ['var(--green)', '1 = accept'],
      ['var(--pink)',  '0 = reject'],
      ['var(--yellow)','M_i on 〈M_i〉'],
      ['var(--cyan)',  'D = ¬diagonal']
    ];
    items.forEach((p,k) => {
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:30+k*150, y:lY-12, width:14, height:14, rx:3,
        fill:'transparent', stroke:p[0], 'stroke-width':1.5}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:48+k*150, y:lY-1,'font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent = p[1];
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });

    // readout
    const diag = Array.from({length:N}, (_,i) => answer(i,i) ? 1 : 0).join(' ');
    const drow = Array.from({length:N}, (_,i) => answer(i,i) ? 0 : 1).join(' ');
    out.textContent =
      `diagonal M_i on 〈M_i〉 : ${diag}\n` +
      `D's row (¬diagonal)   : ${drow}\n\n` +
      `D disagrees with every M_i within budget f, so L(D) ∉ DTIME(f).\n` +
      `D itself runs in g = ${gap}·f time (one extra simulation factor + flip).\n\n` +
      `Hierarchy theorem: when f log f = o(g), DTIME(f) ⊊ DTIME(g).`;
  }

  gapIn.addEventListener('input', render);
  render();
})();
</script>
